revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[arz:فوجيوارا نو سيشى]][[en:Fujiwara no Seishi]][[fa:فوجیوارا نو سیشی]][[fr:Fujiwara no Seishi (972-1025)]][[id:Fujiwara no Seishi]][[ja:藤原娍子]][[ko:후지와라노 세이시 (972년)]][[sv:Fujiwara no Seishi]][[th:ฟุจิวะระ โนะ เซชิ]][[zh:藤原娍子]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=0972|death_date={{death date|1025|4|25}}|birth_place={{ill|Japan|qid=Q17|1=Japan}}|death_place={{ill|Japan|qid=Q17|1=Japan}}|gender={{ill|female|qid=Q6581072|1=Female (gender)}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|qid=Q17|1=Japan}}|occupation={{ill|empress consort|qid=Q7723211|1=Empress consort}}|title={{ill|Empress of Japan|qid=Q595038|1=Empress of Japan}}|spouse={{ill|Sanjō|qid=Q357231|1=Emperor Sanjō}}|children={{ill|Atsuakira-shinnō|qid=Q11499522|1=Atsuakira-shinnō}}; {{ill|Atsuhiro-shinnō|qid=Q17502245|1=Atsuhiro-shinnō}}; {{ill|Atsunori-shinnō|qid=Q17502240|1=Atsunori-shinnō}}; {{ill|Shishi|qid=Q48764156|1=Shishi}}; {{ill|Shōshin-nyūdōshinnō|qid=Q11491592|1=Shōshin-nyūdōshinnō}}; {{ill|Tōshi-naishinnō|qid=Q5354435|1=Tōshi-naishinnō}}|father={{ill|Fujiwara no Naritoki|qid=Q11623387|1=Fujiwara no Naritoki}}|mother={{ill|Minamoto no Nobumitsu's daughter|qid=Q106345791|1=Minamoto no Nobumitsu's daughter}}|relatives={{ill|Fujiwara no Michitō|qid=Q11623581|1=Fujiwara no Michitō}}; {{ill|Fujiwara no Tametō|qid=Q11623391|1=Fujiwara no Tametō}}|family={{ill|Imperial House of Japan|qid=Q909452|1=Imperial House of Japan}}|religion={{ill|Buddhism|qid=Q748|1=Buddhism}}|native_name=藤原娍子}}
{{family name hatnote|Fujiwara|lang=Japanese}} {{Expand Japanese|topic=bio|藤原娍子|date=November 2011}} {{Infobox royalty |name = Fujiwara no Seishi |succession = {{ill|Empress of Japan|en|Empress of Japan|qid=Q595038|lt=Empress consort of Japan|1=Empress of Japan}} |reign = 1012–1016 |consort = yes |spouse = {{ill|Emperor Sanjō|en|Emperor Sanjō|qid=Q357231|lt=Emperor Sanjō|1=Emperor Sanjō}} |issue = {{plainlist| * Prince Atsuakira * Prince Atsunori * Prince Atsuhira * Princess Tōshi * Princess Shishi * Prince Moroakira}} |royal house = {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|qid=Q909452|lt=Imperial House of Japan|1=Imperial House of Japan}} |father = Fujiwara no Naritoki |mother = |birth_date = 972 |birth_place = |death_date = {{death date and age|1025|4|25|972}} |death_place = |burial_date = |burial_place = |}}
'''Fujiwara no Seishi''' (藤原娍子) (972–1025) was the consort of {{ill|Emperor Sanjō|en|Emperor Sanjō|qid=Q357231|lt=Emperor Sanjō|1=Emperor Sanjō}} of Japan.
==Biography== She was the first daughter of [[Fujiwara no Naritoki]] (藤原済時). Her father was the cousin of the regent {{ill|Fujiwara no Michinaga|en|Fujiwara no Michinaga|qid=Q433133|lt=Fujiwara no Michinaga|1=Fujiwara no Michinaga}}.
She was arranged to marry the future Emperor one year after his succession. The Emperor already had an Empress, [[:en:Fujiwara no Kenshi (Sanjō)|Fujiwara no Kenshi]], who was the daughter of the regent Michinaga and cousin of Seishi. However, Fujiwara no Michinaga had introduced the custom of the Emperor having two Empresses: one called ''Chugu'', and the other called ''Kogo''. Fujiwara no Michinaga agreed for Fujiwara no Seishi to be given the title of Empress (as ''Kogo''), but he demonstrated that Seishi was to have lower Rank than his daughter in practice by making sure that virtually no one attended the ceremony in which Seishi was made Empress.<ref>John Whitney Hall, Delmer Myers Brown, Donald H. Shively, William H. McCullough, Marius B. Jansen, Peter Duus, Kōzō Yamamura, ''[https://books.google.com/books?id=eiTWWfoyuyAC&dq=Fujiwara+no+Kenshi+1027+Sanj%C5%8D&pg=PA69 The Cambridge History of Japan, Volym 2]''</ref> When the courtiers were summoned to the elevation ceremony of the second Empress, they laughed at the messengers and gathered at the apartments of the Empress Kenshi instead.<ref>Helen Craig McCullough, ''[https://books.google.com/books?id=2s7_AwAAQBAJ&dq=Fujiwara+no+Seishi+1025&pg=PA354 OKAGAMI, The Great Mirror: Fujiwara Michinaga (966-1027) and His Times]''</ref>
Empress Seishi was reportedly well liked by the Emperor, but she was overshadowed at court by Empress Kenshi, and never managed to exert any influence.<ref>Helen Craig McCullough, ''[https://books.google.com/books?id=2s7_AwAAQBAJ&dq=Fujiwara+no+Seishi+1025&pg=PA354 OKAGAMI, The Great Mirror: Fujiwara Michinaga (966-1027) and His Times]''</ref> She became a nun in 1019, one year after her husband's abdication.
==Issue== *Imperial Prince Atsuakira (敦明親王) (994–1051), [[:en:Emperor Go-Ichijo|Emperor Go-Ichijō]]'s [[:en:Crown Prince|Crown Prince]]; later, ''Ko-ichijō In'' (小一条院) *Imperial Prince Atsunori (敦儀親王) (997–1054) *Imperial Prince Atsuhira (敦平親王) (999–1049) *Imperial Princess ''Tōshi'' (real pronunciation is unknown) (当子内親王) (1001–1023), 37th [[Saiō]] in [[:en:Grand Shrine of Ise|Grand Shrine of Ise]]) 1012–1016 *Imperial Princess ''Shishi'' (real pronunciation is unknown) (禔子内親王) (1003–1048), spouse of {{ill|Fujiwara no Norimichi|en|Fujiwara no Norimichi|qid=Q3090649|lt=Fujiwara no Norimichi|1=Fujiwara no Norimichi}} (藤原教通) *Imperial Prince Moroakira (師明親王) (1005–1085), lay priest under the name Seishin (性信) (second head priest of {{ill|Ninna-ji|en|Ninna-ji|qid=Q1202871|lt=Ninna-ji|1=Ninna-ji}} Temple, 仁和寺)
==Notes== {{reflist}}
{{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[:en:Fujiwara no Shōshi|Fujiwara no Shōshi]] | title={{ill|Empress of Japan|en|Empress of Japan|qid=Q595038|lt=Empress consort of Japan|1=Empress of Japan}} | after={{ill|Fujiwara no Kenshi (994–1027)|en|Fujiwara no Kenshi (994–1027)|qid=Q5507521|lt=Fujiwara no Kenshi|1=Fujiwara no Kenshi (994–1027)}} | years=1012–1016}} {{s-end}}
